title: Internal comms after failed experiment
description: A high-profile experiment failed after 6 weeks of eng work. Write the internal comms that frame the failure as learning, not waste.
scenarioBrief: You are a PM at TestHarbor, a Series A experimentation platform company (86 employees, $5.7M ARR). Your immediate assignment is internal comms after failed experiment. The executive team expects a recommendation before Friday's review. You have four context sources: a KPI snapshot, stakeholder notes, customer voice, and execution risk data. Each source is useful but incomplete, and at least one source is biased or contradictory. Treat credibility with customers as a hard constraint, not a soft preference.

contextMaterials.title: KPI Snapshot (Last 90 Days)
contextMaterials.content:
Monthly active accounts: 26546
New logo adds: 2596
Activation rate: 55%
30-day retention: 42%
NPS: -2 (n=163)
Expansion rate in top cohort: 27%
Quarter-over-quarter activation delta: -3 points
Quarter-over-quarter retention delta: 6 points
Caveat: NPS sample over-indexes power users and excludes the most recent downgraded accounts.

contextMaterials.title: Stakeholder Notes
contextMaterials.content:
CRO (Riya Shah): "Buyers are asking for proof points, not intent. We need visible traction fast."
VP Engineering (Grant Ali): "We can move quickly if every blocker has an owner and fallback path."
Head of Customer Success (Noah Choi): "Escalations spike after handoff because expectations are set too optimistically."
Commercial note: 9 late-stage deals cite this area as a decision factor.
Support note: 52 escalations in the last month mention related pain points.
Bias warning: projected revenue impact is directional and not contract-backed.

contextMaterials.title: Customer Voice (Interview Snippets)
contextMaterials.content:
Cedar Insurance (Operations Lead): "This solved one team workflow immediately, then stalled when we tried to scale."
Arbor Payments (IT Manager): "We needed clearer ownership between implementation and support during rollout."
Helio Health Group (Director of Product): "Give us a narrower path that works consistently and we will scale internally."
Data gap: no week-two cohort notes for downgraded accounts.

contextMaterials.title: Delivery and Risk Snapshot
contextMaterials.content:
Open P1/P2 issues: 14
Median cycle time: 21 days
On-time delivery last quarter: 74%
Shared platform dependencies unresolved: 4
Release rollback events last quarter: 1
Average time to resolve escalations: 61 hours
Contradiction: roadmap shows full utilization, but squads report 10-20% interrupt-driven bandwidth.

submissionFields.hint: Map each audience to the message they need and why.
submissionFields.label: Audience and Intent

submissionFields.hint: Write the core narrative that aligns stakeholders on action.
submissionFields.label: Core Narrative

submissionFields.hint: Predict objections from key stakeholders and how you will respond.
submissionFields.label: Expected Pushback

submissionFields.hint: Draft the memo or all-hands update you would send.
submissionFields.label: Message Draft

submissionFields.hint: Explain how you will collect feedback and close open issues.
submissionFields.label: Follow-up and Feedback Loop

rubric.name: Audience Fit
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is messaging adapted to the needs and incentives of each audience?

rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is communication clear, direct, and decision-oriented?

Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Does it handle disagreement constructively and concretely?

rubric.name: Credibility
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Are claims grounded in evidence with transparent caveats?

expertSolution.content: Executives need decision logic and downside exposure, delivery teams need concrete scope and ownership, and customer-facing teams need timing and expectation framing. Tailor message depth by audience without changing the core narrative.

Recommendation: align on a staged plan because the data is mixed, not broken. Activation is 55% and retention is 42%, while NPS (-2, n=163) and 52 escalations indicate trust risk if execution quality slips.

Expect commercial pressure from Riya Shah around the 9 deal pipeline, technical caution from Grant Ali on dependency risk (4 unresolved), and customer-impact pressure from Noah Choi due to escalation volume. Address each with explicit thresholds and review dates.

Team, we are moving to a bounded plan that prioritizes reliability and measurable customer value. Baseline today is 26546 active accounts, activation 55%, retention 42%, and on-time delivery 74%. We will publish weekly progress and revisit scope at the midpoint checkpoint.

Hold a structured Q&A within 24 hours, capture unresolved questions in a decision log, and assign owner plus due date for each item. Publish weekly updates that close the loop on objections raised by Sales, Engineering, and Customer Success.
